current_implemented_build: V60.3.9.1 TrustMap Render Stability and Core Anchor Correction
patch_type: Render stability and core anchor correction over V60.3.9 rendered Layer 1 asset integration
release_chain_status: V60.3.9.1 is the current implemented build. It preserves the original TrustMap shell, center CyberShield Core, MJC shield/logo, left Operational Trust Score, right Selected Asset Trust Score, radar rings, Layer 2, Layer 3, pan/zoom, and mode controls. It stabilizes Layer 1 rendered asset integration so old flat SVG icons do not flash back after clicks, mode changes, or TrustMap re-renders. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.9.1. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
v60_3_9_1_scope_rule: Preserve the original TrustMap shell, center CyberShield Core, MJC shield/logo, left Operational Trust Score, right Selected Asset Trust Score, radar rings, Layer 2, Layer 3, pan/zoom, and mode controls. Stabilize the Layer 1 rendered asset integration so old flat SVG icons do not flash back after clicks, mode changes, or TrustMap re-renders.
non_negotiable_trustmap_scope: Do not replace the TrustMap page
Do not replace the center CyberShield Core
Do not remove the MJC shield/logo
Do not remove the left Operational Trust Score panel
Do not remove the right-side score area
Do not remove Layer 2 or Layer 3
Do not replace the radar / constellation environment
Only replace the graphical representation of the Layer 1 assets
asset_fallback_rule: If WebP files are missing, V60.3.9.1 uses inline holographic fallback visuals so the TrustMap does not revert to the old one-dimensional icon set.

public_trustmap_language.title: Enterprise Trust Map
public_trustmap_language.subtitle: Decision Trust Universe
public_trustmap_language.rule: Do not expose builder language such as registry-driven, renders from registry, versioned render source, implementation layer, or internal version source in executive-facing TrustMap UI.

trustmap_visual_doctrine: The original TrustMap shell is preserved. The Layer 1 graphical assets use rendered WebP visuals when available and inline holographic fallback visuals when missing. The selected-asset panel explains the hovered or focused Layer 1 asset and sits below the existing right-side score area.
selected_asset_panel_rule: The selected-asset panel belongs below the existing right-side score area. It changes when the user hovers or focuses a Layer 1 graphical asset. It must not replace the score area above it.
score_rule: Left score is the Operational Trust Score for the current scenario/environment. Right score is the Selected Asset Trust Score for the hovered/focused Layer 1 asset. Those scores must not be duplicated unless they intentionally represent the same metric.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context. Settings owns build/version/governance/prototype metadata.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_9_1_changes: keeps V60.3.9 rendered WebP asset paths as the preferred source
adds inline SVG holographic fallback visuals if WebP files are missing
adds a MutationObserver to re-apply Layer 1 assets when the base TrustMap renderer mutates
hides old flat SVG icons inside Layer 1 domains so they cannot flash back during re-render cycles
reduces Layer 1 image footprint to reduce overlap
moves the TrustMap world upward inside the center column
strengthens the CyberShield Core as a shield-shaped MJC/CyberShield trust kernel
adds a blue energy/hover pad under the core
keeps right Selected Asset Trust Score distinct from left Operational Trust Score
keeps selected-asset model explanation below the right score area
keeps Daily / Weekly / Monthly trend redraw behavior
preserves Settings as the place for build/version/governance/prototype metadata

trustmap_render_stability_core_anchor.interaction: Mutation-stabilized Layer 1 asset replacement, selected-asset score and model explanation update on hover and focus
trustmap_render_stability_core_anchor.boundary: Static advisory visual and interaction renderer only. No live scoring, evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.

trustmap_rendered_layer1_assets.interaction: selected-asset score and model explanation update on hover and focus
trustmap_rendered_layer1_assets.boundary: Static advisory visual and interaction renderer only. No live scoring, evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3.10 Rendered Asset Placement and Core Fidelity Tuning
goals: verify old flat icons do not flash back
tune image scale and hover glow
tune right score and selected panel spacing
confirm Layer 2 and Layer 3 behavior remain intact
preserve no-new-top-level-tabs and Settings-only governance metadata

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Rendered WebP assets are preferred but inline fallback visuals are available when missing
Layer 1 asset placement requires browser QA
Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
No live scoring, evidence retrieval, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
